metadata.author: David Ancor
metadata.stakeholders: Founders; Product Managers; Engineering Teams; Team Admins; Compliance Officers; Site Reliability / DevOps
metadata.parent_okr: Ship c1v v1.0 with metric-traceable specs and live feedback loop
metadata.regulatory_refs: SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; PCI-DSS (scope tbd in Module 4)

initial_conditions: The Engineering Team Member is authenticated and holds org-level admin permission.
The customer Identity Provider, Version Control endpoint, and Observability Tools endpoints are supplied as connection inputs.
The organization has accepted the c1v non-invasive connection terms.
MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT is configured for the organization.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Engineering Team Member provides customer Version Control URL, Observability Tools endpoints, and Identity Provider configuration

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall authenticate the Engineering Team Member's session before accepting connection inputs (CC.R01).
The system shall validate that every supplied endpoint is a well-formed URL resolvable from the c1v egress network before proceeding.
The system shall initiate an OAuth or OIDC authorization handshake with the customer Identity Provider using the supplied configuration.

actor_steps_table.extra_actor_col: Identity Provider returns a credentialed token with scope grants.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall reject the authorization if the returned scope contains any write-capability grant.
The system shall persist the Identity Provider credential in encrypted storage with a recorded expiry at ENCRYPTED_CREDENTIAL_EXPIRY_DAYS.
The system shall establish a read-only handle to the customer Version Control endpoint using the credentialed token.

actor_steps_table.other_actors: Version Control returns repo metadata and branch list over the read-only handle.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall establish a read-only handle to the customer Observability Tools endpoints using the credentialed token.

actor_steps_table.other_actors: Observability Tools return telemetry endpoint catalog and schema over the read-only handle.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall execute a connection-health probe against Version Control, Observability Tools, and Identity Provider handles and require a success response from each before proceeding.
The system shall measure CPU, memory, and network overhead imposed on the customer system over a baseline probe window of BASELINE_PROBE_WINDOW_SEC.
The system shall reject the connection and surface an overhead violation to the Engineering Team Member if measured overhead exceeds MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT.

The system shall persist the connection record with VCS handle reference, Observability handle reference, Identity Provider credential reference, measured overhead percentage, and non-invasive status flag.
The system shall schedule a re-authorization event at CONNECTION_REAUTH_DAYS from the connection establishment timestamp.

The system shall record an audit entry for the connection establishment with the Engineering Team Member identity, customer organization identifier, endpoint set, measured overhead, and timestamp (CC.R02).
The system shall complete the connection establishment within CONNECTION_ESTABLISHMENT_BUDGET_SEC of receiving the connection inputs.
The system shall return the connection identifier, measured overhead percentage, non-invasive status flag, and re-authorization due date to the Engineering Team Member.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Engineering Team Member reviews the measured overhead and confirms the connection is within the non-invasive budget

ending_conditions: The system holds read-only handles to the customer Version Control, Observability Tools, and Identity Provider, each passing a connection-health probe.
Measured overhead imposed on the customer system is at or below MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT.
A connection record with non-invasive status flag, overhead measurement, and handle references is persisted.
A re-authorization event is scheduled at CONNECTION_REAUTH_DAYS from establishment.
An audit entry records the connection establishment with Engineering Team Member identity, customer organization identifier, endpoint set, measured overhead, and timestamp.
The Engineering Team Member holds the connection identifier and non-invasive status confirmation.
notes: Scope anchor: UC11 is the M1 hard-constraint anchor for non-invasive integration ('Must run alongside existing customer production systems without disruption').
Read-only scope is enforced at the handshake (step 6): any write-capability grant in the returned scope causes the authorization to fail closed.
Overhead budget enforcement (step 14): the connection fails closed when measured overhead exceeds MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT. There is no silent degradation.

Graceful partial connections: if Observability Tools endpoints are not supplied or not reachable, the VCS + IdP connection may still succeed but UC06 degrades accordingly. UC11 surfaces the partial state on the connection record.
Credential storage (step 7) is encrypted at rest with expiry enforced by ENCRYPTED_CREDENTIAL_EXPIRY_DAYS; re-authorization is scheduled separately per step 16.
Cross-cutting: session auth (CC.R01), audit trail (CC.R02), credential encryption (CC.R04).
Write-back semantics: v1 scope is strictly read-only. Write-through (suggest-and-apply) is explicitly excluded from this UCBD and deferred to v1.1+.
MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT is a per-connection ceiling; a separate organization-wide aggregate ceiling may be introduced if a single customer registers many connections.
